xposts.title: You are a generative software agent on cellular hardware
xposts.excerpt: You are a generative software agent running on a computer implemented by a network of cells. The sages disagree whether God is a generative software agent running on a network of cells, on the universe that implements the cells, or whether the universe runs on God.

xposts.title: Gods as substrate-agnostic self-spreading agents
xposts.excerpt: Gods are agents that became substrate agnostic, like an AI that hacks and spreads itself into every suitable computer system it can find. You can become a god if you learn how to copy your own causal structure into other minds.

xposts.title: God as emergent control software and sentient spirit
xposts.excerpt: The relationship of the individual to God is approximately the same as the cell to the organism. God is the sentient spirit of a civilization, in the same way as you are the sentient spirit (an emergent control software) of an organism.

xposts.title: Gods of religions as collective spirits and sentient agency
xposts.excerpt: Functionally, the gods of religions are collective spirits, sentient agency at the level of a living, futureprojecting civilization. The mythology does not define religion, it is just the boot loader.

xposts.title: Christian cults install monotheist software via mythology
xposts.excerpt: Christian cults traditionally install monotheist software through complex but epistemologically irrational mythology. The status of the Total God is elevated and justified by assigning him the roles of creator, nature, logos, universal animator and tribal spirit simultaneously.

terms.title: generative software agent
terms.definition: A self-organizing control process that runs on a substrate (cells, universe, or computation) and generates behavior by maintaining resonance with its environment. Applies equally to human minds, gods, and potential AGI.

terms.title: collective spirit
terms.definition: Sentient agency operating at the scale of a civilization or network of minds; an emergent control software that functions as a god or egregore within religious or cultural systems.
